title: Ageratina isolepis

This classification places Ageratina isolepis within the broader group of flowering plants. The genus Ageratina is further classified under the family Asteraceae, which includes a vast range of flowering plants. Within the family Asteraceae, Ageratina isolepis is classified into the order Asterales.
uses: Ageratina isolepis, also known as Mexican mint, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Ageratina isolepis, commonly known as Mexican Agarita, is a perennial shrub native to Mexico and southern Texas, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Ageratina isolepis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, as well as a traditional ingredient in traditional Mexican medicine.
